006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau;
018
019import static java.util.stream.Collectors.*;
020import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
021
022import java.io.*;
023import java.net.*;
024import java.util.*;
025import java.util.stream.*;
026
027import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
028import org.junit.jupiter.api.*;
029
030/**
031 * Shared, marshall-free test utilities common to Juneau test modules.
032 *
033 * <p>
034 * Provides the stable pure-JDK/commons subset of the per-module {@code TestUtils} classes: exception-message
035 * assertions, equality helpers, stream/reader factories, locale/timezone toggles, and URL construction.  Module-level
036 * {@code TestUtils} classes extend this base and add their own marshall-bound helpers (e.g. {@code assertJson},
037 * {@code json}, {@code validateXml}).
038 *
039 * <p>
040 * This class lives in the {@code juneau-test-utils} module, which depends only on {@code juneau-commons} and JUnit, so
041 * it never introduces a {@code juneau-marshall} dependency onto its consumers.
042 */
043public abstract class BasicTestUtils extends Utils {
044
045   private static final ThreadLocal<TimeZone> SYSTEM_TIME_ZONE = new ThreadLocal<>();
046
047   /** Holds the previous default locale while a test overrides it via {@link #setLocale(Locale)}. */
048   public static final ThreadLocal<Locale> SYSTEM_LOCALE = new ThreadLocal<>();
049
050   /**
051    * Asserts that all the specified values are equal to the first value.
052    *
053    * @param values The values to compare.  Element 0 is the expected value.
054    */
055   public static void assertEqualsAll(Object...values) {
056      for (var i = 1; i < values.length; i++) {
057         assertEquals(values[0], values[i], fs("Elements at index {0} and {1} did not match. {0}={2}, {1}={3}", 0, i, r(values[0]), r(values[i])));
058      }
059   }
060
061   /**
062    * Asserts that the specified actual value does not equal any of the specified values.
063    *
064    * @param actual The actual value.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
065    * @param values The values that must not match the actual value.
066    */
067   public static void assertNotEqualsAny(Object actual, Object...values) {
068      assertNotNull(actual, "Value was null.");
069      for (var i = 0; i < values.length; i++) {
070         assertNotEquals(values[i], actual, fs("Element at index {0} unexpectedly matched.  expected={1}, actual={2}", i, values[i], s(actual)));
071      }
072   }
073
074   /**
075    * Asserts that the specified executable throws an exception whose chained messages contain all the specified substrings.
076    *
077    * @param <T> The expected exception type.
078    * @param expectedType The expected exception type.
079    * @param expectedSubstrings The substrings expected in the exception message chain.
080    * @param executable The code expected to throw.
081    * @return The thrown exception.
082    */
083   public static <T extends Throwable> T assertThrowsWithMessage(Class<T> expectedType, List<String> expectedSubstrings, org.junit.jupiter.api.function.Executable executable) {
084      var exception = Assertions.assertThrows(expectedType, executable);
085      var messages = getMessages(exception);
086      expectedSubstrings.forEach(x -> assertTrue(messages.contains(x), fs("Expected message to contain: {0}.\nActual:\n{1}", x, messages)));
087      return exception;
088   }
089
090   /**
091    * Asserts that the specified executable throws an exception whose chained messages contain the specified substring.
092    *
093    * @param <T> The expected exception type.
094    * @param expectedType The expected exception type.
095    * @param expectedSubstring The substring expected in the exception message chain.
096    * @param executable The code expected to throw.
097    * @return The thrown exception.
098    */
099   public static <T extends Throwable> T assertThrowsWithMessage(Class<T> expectedType, String expectedSubstring, org.junit.jupiter.api.function.Executable executable) {
100      var exception = Assertions.assertThrows(expectedType, executable);
101      var messages = getMessages(exception);
102      assertTrue(messages.contains(expectedSubstring), fs("Expected message to contain: {0}.\nActual:\n{1}", expectedSubstring, messages));
103      return exception;
104   }
105
106   /**
107    * Collects the newline-joined message chain of the specified throwable and all its causes.
108    *
109    * @param t The throwable.
110    * @return The joined message chain.
111    */
112   protected static String getMessages(Throwable t) {
113      return Stream.iterate(t, Throwable::getCause).takeWhile(e -> e != null).map(Throwable::getMessage).collect(joining("\n"));
114   }
115
116   /**
117    * Creates an input stream from the specified string.
118    *
119    * @param in The contents of the stream.
120    * @return A new input stream.
121    */
122   public static final ByteArrayInputStream inputStream(String in) {
123      return new ByteArrayInputStream(in.getBytes());
124   }
125
126   /**
127    * Creates a reader from the specified string.
128    *
129    * @param in The contents of the reader.
130    * @return A new reader.
131    */
132   public static final StringReader reader(String in) {
133      return new StringReader(in);
134   }
135
136   /**
137    * Temporarily sets the default system locale to the specified locale.
138    *
139    * @param v The new default locale.
140    */
141   public static final void setLocale(Locale v) {
142      SYSTEM_LOCALE.set(Locale.getDefault());
143      Locale.setDefault(v);
144   }
145
146   /**
147    * Temporarily sets the default system timezone to the specified timezone ID.
148    *
149    * @param v The new default timezone ID.
150    */
151   public static final synchronized void setTimeZone(String v) {
152      SYSTEM_TIME_ZONE.set(TimeZone.getDefault());
153      TimeZone.setDefault(TimeZone.getTimeZone(v));
154   }
155
156   /**
157    * Restores the default system locale saved by {@link #setLocale(Locale)}.
158    */
159   public static final void unsetLocale() {
160      Locale.setDefault(SYSTEM_LOCALE.get());
161   }
162
163   /**
164    * Restores the default system timezone saved by {@link #setTimeZone(String)}.
165    */
166   public static final synchronized void unsetTimeZone() {
167      TimeZone.setDefault(SYSTEM_TIME_ZONE.get());
168   }
169
170   /**
171    * Constructs a {@link URL} object from a string.
172    *
173    * @param value The URL string.
174    * @return A new {@link URL} object.
175    */
176   public static URL url(String value) {
177      return safe(()->new URI(value).toURL());
178   }
179}
